concerns.text: T024 calculates IQR bounds and T025 logs the count, but no task explicitly implements the filtering logic to EXCLUDE outliers from the dataset before the Mann-Whitney U test (T026). FR-005 mandates exclusion; the current task list leaves the implementer guessing whether to filter in-place or pass a subset to T026.

T019 states 'perform manual spot-check' but omits the implementation of the 'stratified random sample' logic required by FR-011. The task does not specify how to stratify (e.g., by repo, by PR size) or how to randomly select n=50, making the sampling method non-deterministic.

T027 mentions 'abort primary test' if AI group < 30, but lacks a concrete deliverable: it does not specify writing a 'limitation flag' to the output JSON (T029) or raising a specific exception. The implementer cannot deterministically know how to signal this abort condition to the reporting phase (T034).

T035 requires injecting a limitation statement if false-negative rate > 10%, but does not specify the exact text of the statement or the variable name holding the rate. The implementer must guess the string format, violating the 'concrete deliverable' rule.

T001 'Create project structure per implementation plan' is too coarse. It covers an entire phase of file creation. The atomizer will split this, but it should be flagged here as a single task covering multiple file creations (requirements.txt, code/, data/, etc.).

T039 'Code cleanup and refactoring' is too coarse and vague. It lacks a concrete deliverable or verification method (e.g., 'Run ruff --fix and ensure zero errors'). This is a catch-all task that should be split or removed.

T012 mentions fetching 'top 10 Python/JS repos' but does not specify the exact API endpoint or query parameters (e.g., 'sort=stars&order=desc&per_page=10&language=Python'). The implementer cannot deterministically execute this without guessing the API call details.
